message.content:
Run curl -sSL https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/releases/download/v0.78.4/terragrunt_linux_amd64 -o terragrunt
/home/runner/_work/_temp/29777a48-aec8-430d-afc9-6eb1e699aa19.sh: line 4: /usr/local/bin/terragrunt: cannot execute binary file: Exec format error
Error: Process completed with exit code 126.

content.text: The error indicates the runner is downloading `terragrunt_linux_amd64` but the GitHub Actions runner is likely ARM64 architecture. Let me check your CI configuration.

     6→    branches: [main]

file.content:
name: tf-aws-env ci/cd

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [synchronize, opened, reopened]
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

concurrency: tf-aws-env-lock

permissions:
  contents: read
  issues: write
  pull-requests: write
  id-token: write

env:
  AWS_REGION: eu-west-1
  TFLINT_VERSION: '0.50.3'
  TERRAFORM_VERSION: '1.12.2'
  TERRAGRUNT_VERSION: '0.78.4'
  WORKING_DIRECTORY: tg-configs

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint and Setup
    runs-on: gha-runner-scale-set
    timeout-minutes: 10
    outputs:
      tg-configs: ${{ steps.sorted-ls.outputs.labeled-dirs }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v2
        with:
         terraform_version: ${{ env.TERRAFORM_VERSION }}

      - name: Install Terragrunt
        run: |
          curl -sSL https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/releases/download/v${{ env.TERRAGRUNT_VERSION }}/terragrunt_linux_amd64 -o terragrunt

content.text: The issue is clear. The `lint` job hardcodes `terragrunt_linux_amd64`, but the runner (`gha-runner-scale-set`) appears to be ARM64. The fix is to detect the architecture dynamically.

input.old_string:
- name: Install Terragrunt
        run: |
          curl -sSL https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/releases/download/v${{ env.TERRAGRUNT_VERSION }}/terragrunt_linux_amd64 -o terragrunt
          chmod +x terragrunt
          sudo mv terragrunt /usr/local/bin/
          terragrunt --version
input.new_string:
- name: Install Terragrunt
        run: |
          ARCH=$(uname -m)
          case "$ARCH" in
            x86_64) TG_ARCH="amd64" ;;
            aarch64|arm64) TG_ARCH="arm64" ;;
            *) echo "Unsupported architecture: $ARCH" && exit 1 ;;
          esac
          curl -sSL https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/releases/download/v${{ env.TERRAGRUNT_VERSION }}/terragrunt_linux_${TG_ARCH} -o terragrunt
          chmod +x terragrunt
          sudo mv terragrunt /usr/local/bin/
          terragrunt --version

Yes, **dev and qa `terragrunt.hcl` files are essentially identical**. The dev file already has all the changes from QA — `cluster_version = "1.31"`, same addon versions, same node group config, everything.

The only minor whitespace differences exist (extra blank line in dev's `vpc-cni` block, trailing blank line before closing `cluster_addons` brace), but functionally they are the same.

So your DEV branch already has the EKS 1.30→1.31 upgrade changes that were applied to QA.
